name: KLIPPEL-TRÉNAUNAY SYNDROME
definition: rare sporadic (nonhereditary) combined capillary-venous malformation of trunk + extremities

demographics.Location: one lower limb in 75-95% (10-15 x more common than upper extremity); bilateral

distribution: one lower limb in 75-95% (10-15 x more common than upper extremity); bilateral
discriminating_features: circumferential soft-tissue hypertrophy (at birth / later in life)

general: circumferential soft-tissue hypertrophy (at birth / later in life)
phleboliths in pelvis (bowel wall, anterior wall of urinary bladder) ← prior hemorrhage /
slow uptake of contrast material in visceral vascular malformations during delayed phase
aplasia / hypoplasia of lower extremity veins (18-40%): ? selective flow of contrast
incompetent valveless collateral venous channels (? persistent lateral limb bud vein =
deep venous malformations of femoral vein (common)

clinical.Cx: stasis dermatitis, thrombophlebitis, cellulitis, deep venous thrombosis, pulmonary
